title: cyathea axillaris
intro: Cyathea axillaris, also known as the palm fern, is a species of tree fern native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its tall, slender stem and fronds that resemble palm leaves. The medicinal properties of Cyathea axillaris include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Cyathea axillaris can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
